concerns.text: Tasks T026–T029 (Experience Trace, Style vs. Substance, Incoherence Tolerance, Turing Test) are defined in the tasks but have no corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) or Success Criterion (SC) in spec.md. These represent unapproved scope additions that violate the 'Single Source of Truth' principle (Constitution Principle IV) and lack traceability to the research design.

User Story 1 requires generation across 'two open-source LLM checkpoints', but the tasks only implement T009 (TinyLlama) and T012 (optional local 7B). There is no task explicitly covering the generation of the second checkpoint as a primary dataset requirement for the automated pipeline, leaving the US1 acceptance criteria unmet for the CI path.

Task T011 (Control Corpus) references 'KR-001' (Key Result), but no Key Results are defined in spec.md or plan.md. The task lacks a valid traceability tag to a spec requirement (FR/SC) or plan element, making its necessity and scope unverified against the current design.

Task T022 is tagged [US3] but implements 'sensitivity analysis on Cohen's κ threshold' which is explicitly defined as FR-011 (linked to US-2 in spec.md). The task is mis-tagged to the wrong User Story, creating a traceability mismatch between the task and the functional requirement it implements.
